codeReview.summary: The implementation adds a coherent color accent system (blue #0055FF, orange #FF3300, green #00AA00, red #CC0000) to form labels, tabs, result cards, and status indicators. Brutalist constraints are properly maintained — no border-radius, gradients, or box-shadow introduced. The code structure is clean: CSS classes for winner/alternative on result cards, data-tab attribute selectors for tab styling, and ::before pseudo-elements ensure status info is not conveyed by color alone. However, two color combinations fail WCAG AA contrast requirements, which is a blocking issue per VAL-COLOR-002.

issues.description: WCAG AA contrast failure: .result-recommendation.alternative uses #FF3300 background with #fff text. Computed contrast ratio is 3.67:1. At font-size 0.75rem (12px) and font-weight 700, this text does NOT qualify as 'large text' (needs ≥14pt/18.67px bold or ≥18pt/24px regular). WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text. Fix: darken the orange (e.g., #CC2900 gives ~5.0:1) or use dark text on the orange background.

WCAG AA contrast failure: .tx-status.success uses #00AA00 text on #e8e8e8 background. Computed contrast ratio is 2.54:1, which fails even the large text threshold of 3:1. The worker calculated contrast against white (4.57:1) but the actual CSS background is #e8e8e8 (gray). Fix: darken the green (e.g., #007700 gives ~4.7:1 on #e8e8e8) or change the background to #fff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS.md documents the color palette as 'black (#000) / white (#fff) + #0055FF accent' under 'Brutalist design rules', but the implementation now uses four accent colors (#0055FF, #FF3300, #00AA00, #CC0000). The AGENTS.md should be updated to reflect the expanded palette so future workers know which colors are available and don't introduce additional ones.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line: 'Brutalist design rules: black (#000) / white (#fff) + #0055FF accent, no border-radius anywhere'. Compare to src/server.ts line 369: '/* Color Palette: Black/White + Blue accent (#0055FF) + Orange accent (#FF3300) + Green (#00AA00) + Red (#CC0000) */'

sharedStateObservations.observation: No library entry documents the UI color system with WCAG contrast ratios against their intended backgrounds. The worker calculated #00AA00 contrast against white (4.57:1) but the actual background is #e8e8e8, leading to a contrast failure. A color-system.md library entry listing each accent color, its intended backgrounds, and verified contrast ratios would prevent this class of error for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript planning phase states '#00AA00 (green) for success states - WCAG AA compliant (4.57:1 on white)' but .tx-status.success actually has background: #e8e8e8, yielding 2.54:1 contrast.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not run 'npm run lint' during verification, though the fullstack-worker skill explicitly requires it: 'npm run lint # Must pass (run lint:fix if needed)'. Worker ran typecheck and test but skipped lint. The deviation was not flagged in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification.commandsRun lists only: npm run typecheck, npm test, curl health check. Skill SKILL.md section 4 says 'Run ALL of these and fix any issues: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test'.

summary: The color accent implementation is structurally sound and maintains brutalist design constraints (no border-radius, gradients, or shadows). The four-color system (blue, orange, green, red) is applied consistently to form labels, tabs, result cards, and status indicators. However, two color combinations fail WCAG AA contrast requirements: (1) the ALTERNATIVE badge (#FF3300 on #fff = 3.67:1, needs 4.5:1 for 12px text), and (2) the success status text (#00AA00 on #e8e8e8 = 2.54:1, fails even the 3:1 large-text threshold). These are blocking failures against VAL-COLOR-002. Both are fixable by darkening the respective colors or adjusting backgrounds.
